concepts.title: eSIM Management
concepts.summary: The centralized admin interface within the Zoom web portal for managing Zoom Phone Mobile eSIMs. Admins can create eSIM packages for users, issue and manage eSIMs, view roaming details, and control activation states. It provides a single pane of glass for mobile connectivity administration.
key_points: Accessible under Phone System Management > eSIM Management
Displays users, packages, numbers, status, roaming details, and available actions
Search bar for finding specific users
Ellipsis menu per user row for actions like View, Issue new eSIM

concepts.title: eSIM Package
concepts.summary: The combination of a Zoom Phone user, a Zoom Phone Mobile package (license type), and a phone number that constitutes a fully provisioned eSIM service. Creating a package automatically provisions the subscriber, generates eSIM details, and triggers a welcome email with a QR code.
key_points: Three components: user, package (license tier), and phone number
Auto-provisions subscriber and eSIM on creation
Triggers welcome email with QR code for user activation
Can be disabled, enabled, or reissued by admin

concepts.title: eSIM Reissuance
concepts.summary: The process of issuing a new eSIM for a user, which deactivates the previous eSIM and generates a new QR code welcome email. Used when a user loses their device or an eSIM needs to be regenerated.
key_points: Previous eSIM is automatically deactivated
New QR code sent via email
Available from both list view (Action column) and user detail page
Requires admin confirmation in a pop-up modal

concepts.title: International Roaming Day Pass
concepts.summary: A time-bounded roaming add-on that admins can enable per user. Roaming is disabled by default and operates on a day-pass model: the pass is only activated when the user actually starts roaming, not at purchase time. Admins can set start and end dates per roaming plan.
key_points: Disabled by default for all new eSIM packages
Day pass activates on actual roaming, not on purchase
Date range specified by admin (start and end dates)
Can be added, edited, or deleted per user
Helps limit unintended roaming charges

user_roles.title: Account Owner
user_roles.summary: Has full access to eSIM Management to create eSIM packages, manage eSIM lifecycle (issue, deactivate, enable), control roaming, and view user details. Can provision new users and manage all aspects of mobile connectivity.
key_points: Full access to eSIM Management under Phone System Management
Can create, view, edit, deactivate, and reissue eSIM packages
Manages international roaming plans (add, edit, delete)
Requires privilege to edit account settings

user_roles.title: Admin
user_roles.summary: Admins with the privilege to edit account settings can access eSIM Management and perform the same operations as account owners: creating packages, managing eSIMs, and controlling roaming. Same license prerequisites apply.
key_points: Must have privilege to edit account settings
Full eSIM Management capabilities matching account owner
Requires Zoom Phone Mobile license per user to provision

task_flows.title: Access eSIM Management
task_flows.summary: Admins navigate to eSIM Management via Phone System Management in the navigation menu. The landing page displays a table of provisioned users with their packages, numbers, status, roaming details, and available row actions.
key_points: Navigation: Phone System Management > eSIM Management
Landing page: tabular view with user, package, number, status, roaming, and actions columns
Search bar at top for user lookup
Ellipsis menu and action buttons per row

task_flows.title: Create an eSIM Package
task_flows.summary: Admins create a new eSIM package by selecting a user, a package tier, and a phone number from dropdowns in a pop-up modal. On save, the system auto-provisions the subscriber, generates eSIM details, and sends a welcome email with a QR code to the user.
key_points: Accessed via Create button in top-right corner of eSIM Management
Three dropdown selections: Username, Package, Number
Phone number must already exist in the account
Auto-provisioning on save: subscriber created, eSIM generated, QR email sent

task_flows.title: View and Manage User eSIM Details
task_flows.summary: Admins drill into a specific user's eSIM details by clicking the ellipsis menu and selecting View. The detail page shows General Info (user name, package, number, status, roaming details) and provides action buttons at the top (Issue new eSIM, Deactivate, Enable).
key_points: Access via ellipsis > View on the eSIM Management list
Detail page organized into action bar and General Info section
Editable fields: user name, phone number (Edit buttons inline)
Roaming details sub-panel with Add, Edit, Delete actions

task_flows.title: Issue a New eSIM (Reissuance)
task_flows.summary: When a user loses their device or an eSIM needs replacement, admins click 'Issue new eSIM' from either the list view or detail page. A confirmation modal warns that the previous eSIM will be deactivated. On confirm, a new eSIM is generated and a welcome email with new QR code is sent.
key_points: Two access points: Action column in list view, or action bar in detail page
Confirmation modal with warning about previous eSIM deactivation
Triggers new QR code email to user
Use case: lost/stolen devices, or eSIM regeneration

task_flows.title: Manage International Roaming
task_flows.summary: Admins control roaming for each user from the user detail page. They can add a new roaming plan with start and end dates, edit existing plan dates, or delete active roaming service. Roaming is disabled by default for all new packages.
key_points: Add roaming: click Add International Roaming, set start/end dates, confirm
Edit roaming: click Edit on existing entry, modify dates, confirm
Delete roaming: ellipsis > Delete on existing entry, confirm deletion
Date picker UI for start/end dates
Day pass only activates on actual roaming

constraints.title: eSIM Management Access Prerequisites
constraints.summary: Accessing and operating eSIM Management requires account owner or admin privileges with permission to edit account settings. Additionally, each user being provisioned must have a Zoom Phone Mobile license, and their device must be a compatible eSIM-enabled smartphone.
key_points: Account owner or admin with edit account settings privilege
Zoom Phone Mobile license required per user
Compatible eSIM-enabled smartphone (iOS or Android) required
Verified business address required for mobile eSIM numbers

constraints.title: Irreversible eSIM Deactivation
constraints.summary: When an admin deactivates a user's eSIM, the action cannot be undone. This is explicitly noted in the UI. Design implication: a clear, unambiguous warning modal should precede this destructive action.
key_points: Deactivation is permanent and irreversible
Design must include explicit warning before confirming
Consider secondary confirmation pattern for destructive actions

constraints.title: Previous eSIM Deactivation on Reissuance
constraints.summary: Issuing a new eSIM automatically deactivates the previous eSIM. This is an important side effect admins must understand before reissuing, especially if the original device is not actually lost.
key_points: New eSIM issuance renders previous eSIM inactive
Only one active eSIM per user at a time
Design should clearly communicate this in the confirmation modal

constraints.title: Roaming Default-Off and Date-Bounded Access
constraints.summary: International roaming is disabled by default for all new eSIM packages. Admins must explicitly enable it and set an end date. The day pass only activates when the user actually begins roaming, not at the time of admin configuration.
key_points: Default-off: admin must opt in per user
End date required to limit unintended charges
Day pass activated by user's actual roaming behavior, not admin action
Design implication: clearly display roaming status and dates in user detail

ux_patterns.title: Ellipsis Menu for Row-Level Actions
ux_patterns.summary: Each user row in the eSIM Management list uses an ellipsis menu to surface secondary actions (e.g., View). This keeps the list view clean while making contextual actions discoverable.
key_points: Ellipsis icon per table row
Clicking reveals contextual menu options
Used for drill-down navigation (View) rather than inline editing

ux_patterns.title: Pop-Up Modal for Create and Confirmation Flows
ux_patterns.summary: Creating eSIM packages, issuing new eSIMs, and managing roaming dates all use pop-up modal windows. This pattern keeps the admin anchored in context while performing focused tasks.
key_points: Create user flow: pop-up with dropdown fields (Username, Package, Number)
Confirmation modals for destructive actions (deactivate, delete roaming)
Roaming date edits: pop-up with date picker fields

ux_patterns.title: Action Bar with Primary and Destructive Buttons
ux_patterns.summary: The user detail page presents a set of action buttons at the top (Issue new eSIM, Deactivate, Enable), clearly separating primary actions from destructive ones. The irreversible Deactivate action is noted explicitly.
key_points: Top action bar with multiple action buttons
Deactivate noted as irreversible
Enable/Deactivate toggle pattern based on current state

ux_patterns.title: Inline Edit in Detail Sections
ux_patterns.summary: Within the General Info section of the user detail page, editable fields (user name, phone number) feature an Edit button that enables inline editing rather than navigating to a separate form.
key_points: Edit buttons inline next to field labels
Limited to specific fields (user name, phone number)
Read-only fields (Package, Status) have no Edit button

ux_patterns.title: Automatic QR Code Email on Provisioning
ux_patterns.summary: Creating an eSIM package or issuing a new eSIM automatically triggers a welcome email containing a QR code to the user. This zero-touch pattern delegates device setup to the end user while keeping the admin informed that provisioning is complete.
key_points: QR code delivered via email automatically
No admin intervention needed after creation
User self-service activation reduces support burden
